fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer audiologist license to indiana, audiologist compact indiana, indiana audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Board
board.mailing_address: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, 402 West Washington Street, Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204
board.physical_address: 402 W. Washington St., Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Indiana.

steps.title: Register and Complete Online Application
steps.description: Create an account on MyLicense.IN.gov and complete the Audiologist license application form online, providing all requested personal and professional information.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official transcripts, verifying your Master's or Doctoral degree in Audiology from an accredited program, to be sent directly to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

steps.title: Verify National Examination Scores
steps.description: Request your official passing scores for the national examination in audiology (Praxis) to be sent directly from the testing service to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

steps.title: Provide Professional Certification Verification
steps.description: Have verification of your current Certificate of Clinical Competence in Audiology (CCC-A) from ASHA or Board Certified in Audiology from AAA sent directly to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

steps.title: Pass the Indiana Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete and pass the Indiana Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically accessible as part of the online application process or through a link provided by the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Indiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Indiana
hero.dek: Audiologist license applicants in Indiana must apply online through the official MyLicense.IN.gov portal, which is managed by the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to MyLicense.IN.gov, click 'Register a new User Account', complete the registration form with your personal details, email, and create a username and password. Once registered and logged in, select 'Professional Licensing' and then 'Apply for a new license' to begin your application for an Audiologist license.
